title: erigeron thunbergii subsp. glabratus
intro: Erigeron thunbergii subsp. glabratus, also known as Glabrous Fleabane, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Glabrous Fleabane include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the safety and efficacy of its uses.
